Higher resolution screens are capable of producing sharper, clearer, more detailed images. You can choose any resolution you desire.
Tweak some options and make your gameplay experience even better! You can change between categories using the buttons on the top.

VSync is the graphics technology responsible for synchronizing the frame rate of a game to the refresh rate of a monitor. This synchronization delivers smooth, uninterrupted gameplay for graphics-heavy 3D games.
Limit the frame rate. It is recommended to match this option with the refresh rate of your monitor. Note that this option has no effect when VSync is enabled.
Select the preperred window mode. If you're experiencing input lags , you can try the Fullscreen mode.

Higher quality textures result in significantly deeper details, but also require more VRAM and video processing hardware.
Texture filtering makes distant objects in games look sharper. It works by improving the appearance of textures viewed at a steep angle.

Shop button description here. Supports multi-line.
Low is the fastest but has lower image quality. High pays more attention to picture quality while still being able to increase the frame rate.

Adjusts the amount and quality of grass.
Lower values improve performance.
Higher values increase visual quality but may reduce performance.
The lower the quality, the faster the performance.
Higher values make the image clearer, while lower values improve performance.

Array.key: KNIGHT CLASS  |  ORDER OF THE SUNKEN LIGHT
Array.value: KNIGHT CLASS  |  ORDER OF THE SUNKEN LIGHT

A drifting citadel of rusted steel and broken stone. The Phantom Train docks, survivors barter Voidmarks, and the wind carries prayers to gods that no longer answer. The Black Sea is rising. Its cold tide will soon submerge the Nexus.

Ruined circle of world gates hanging in torn sky. The radioscope scans and wakes things best left sleeping. Xibal feeds from the hub as Chaac bends lightning through the nodes. Move fast or the paths seal and the array becomes a tomb.

The multiverse lies shattered. The Void spills through a broken sky. Thrones stand empty.

The Nexus Hold drifts across an endless black sea. Iron groans. Steam whispers through hollow halls. One road remains between dying worlds—the Phantom Train.

Its furnace burns power taken from fallen lords.

Machine keepers guard the fading lights. Survivors trade rumors and relics. Along the rails the Archons appear without warning. Their gifts carry a price. Their rivalries are older than memory.

Across broken worlds lie ruined cities and ancient battlefields. Rusted armor, torn banners, shattered rifles and blades. Parasite husks rot while revenant machines roam beneath a sky that never wakes.

Above the ruins rules a horned tyrant who claims this shard of reality.

Defeat such rulers and the Phantom Train howls onward each victory a faint step toward Elysium.

Name your hero, wanderer. This name appears on global leaderboards. You can change it in Options | General Settings.

Thanks for supporting Dark Light: Survivor.

This build gives you an early look at the game, with all 3 Early Access maps and 3 playable classes available to try.

As this is still an Early Access build, you may encounter bugs. We are updating the game regularly and continuing to add new features.

We are a small indie team, and your feedback means a lot to us. If you enjoy the game, please share your thoughts in the community and follow the project.

Please note that balance, content, and other parts of the game may continue to change during Early Access.
